global_cues.us_markets: US markets closed at fresh record highs on Apr 22: S&P 500 +1.05% to 7,137.90, Nasdaq +1.64% to 24,657.57, Dow +0.69% (+340.65 pts) to 49,490.03. Rally driven by Trump's indefinite extension of US-Iran ceasefire and tech leadership.
global_cues.european_markets: European stocks closed lower Apr 22 as traders digested Iran ceasefire extension and mixed earnings. Germany halved 2026 GDP growth forecast to 0.5% citing Middle East conflict and Strait of Hormuz disruption.
global_cues.asian_markets: Mixed to weak. GIFT Nifty trading at ~24,430 (+0.30%) indicating flat-to-positive Indian open. Nikkei 225 down 1.75% to 58,475, Hang Seng down 0.89% to 26,160.

fii_dii_summary: Both institutional flows negative on Apr 22: FIIs net sellers ₹2,078.36 Cr; DIIs net sellers ₹1,048.17 Cr. Broader trend shows FIIs have pulled ~₹1.62 lakh crore since US-Iran conflict began due to oil spike, weaker rupee, and hedging costs. Selling pressure persists.
earnings_calendar: Infosys - Q4 FY26 results Apr 23 (~3:45 PM IST); FY27 guidance is key market-moving number; PAT consensus ~₹7,700 Cr
HCL Technologies - already reported: PAT ₹4,488 Cr (+4.2% YoY), revenue ₹33,981 Cr (+12.3% YoY)
Axis Bank - Q4 results this week
Reliance Industries - Q4 results this week
Aurobindo Pharma - ₹800 Cr buyback opens Apr 23 at ₹1,475/share (closes Apr 29)
macro_events: RBI MPC (Apr 8) held repo rate at 5.25% for second consecutive meeting; neutral stance maintained
RBI FY27 GDP projection 6.9%; CPI inflation projection 4.6% (core 4.4%)
RBI flagged upside inflation risks from energy prices and weather; World Bank sees FY27 CPI at 4.9%
RBI directed state oil importers to use SBI special credit facility for FX, easing rupee pressure
Regulatory reforms: eased CRAR computation, IFR removal for some banks, rationalised board governance
sector_themes: IT: Infosys Q4 print + FY27 guidance is the swing factor; HCL Tech showed resilient growth (revenue +12.3% YoY)
Banking: SBI leadership reshuffle (8 new DMDs effective Apr 21); Karnataka Bank-Pine Labs digital payments tie-up
Pharma: Aurobindo Pharma ₹800 Cr buyback at ₹1,475 (premium) opens Apr 23 - positive signalling
Defence/Metals: BEML secured ₹590 Cr MoD order for trawl assemblies
Oil-sensitive sectors (paints, airlines, tyres, OMCs) remain under pressure from elevated crude
risk_flags: US-Iran ceasefire still conditional; any collapse could spike Brent above $100 again (currently ~$96.50, has tested $115)
USD/INR at ~92.6 - rupee capped by Iran uncertainty despite RBI interventions via SBI facility
Record FII outflows (~₹1.62 lakh crore cumulative since conflict) signal fragile foreign sentiment
Strait of Hormuz disruption risk; India is highly oil-sensitive on CAD, inflation, and INR
DII selling joining FII selling on Apr 22 removes domestic cushion - watch for follow-through selling
European growth downgrades (Germany GDP halved) could weigh on export-oriented Indian sectors
brief_summary: Mixed-to-cautious open expected despite strong US record-high close, as Apr 22 saw both FII and DII selling (~₹3,100 Cr combined) and Asian markets trading weak. All eyes on Infosys Q4 result and FY27 guidance at ~3:45 PM IST - this is the single biggest intraday catalyst and will drive IT sector and index direction. Key overhangs: Iran ceasefire fragility, ~$96 Brent, and USD/INR at 92.6; any oil spike or rupee slip could reignite FII outflows.
